title: Writing: Applying Formality and Possession
learningGoal: Construct sentences using the correct level of formality and the accurately gendered possessive pronoun.
instructions: Translate the sentences. Pay extremely close attention to the gender of the object being owned, and the social status of the person you are talking to.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi:
1. Where is your car? (Speaking formally to an elder. Car = Gaadi [Fem])
2. This is my house. (House = Ghar [Masc])
3. Her book is here. (Speaking casually about a girl far away. Book = Kitaab [Fem])
4. Where are you? (Speaking casually to a friend of same age)
promptGoal: Accurately apply aapki, mera, uski, and tum.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: आपकी गाड़ी कहाँ है? यह मेरा घर है। उसकी किताब यहाँ है। तुम कहाँ हो?
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Aapki gaadi kahan hai? Yeh mera ghar hai. Uski kitaab yahaan hai. Tum kahan ho?
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Where is your car? This is my house. Her book is here. Where are you?
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect mapping of possessive endings to the object's gender (Aapki gaadi, Mera ghar, Uski kitaab). Correct use of Tum with 'ho'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: आपकी गाड़ी कहाँ है? यह मेरा घर है। उसकी किताब यहाँ है। तुम कहाँ हो?
acceptableAlternates: Aapki gaadi kahan hai? Yeh mera ghar hai. Uski kitaab yahaan hai. Tum kahan ho?
aapki gaadi kahan hai? yeh mera ghar hai. uski kitaab yahaan hai. tum kahan ho?
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict check on a/e/i endings.

criteria.name: Possessive Gender Failure
criteria.description: If user writes 'Aapka gaadi' or 'Meri ghar', score drops significantly.

feedbackRules.onPass: Phenomenal! You handled the gendered possessives flawlessly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 2. 'Gaadi' is feminine, so 'Your car' must be 'Aapki gaadi', not 'Aapka gaadi'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Check your final sentence. 'Tum' (You casual) must ALWAYS end with the verb 'ho' (Tum kahan ho?).

hintText: Before writing the pronoun (My, Your, His), look at the word immediately following it. If it's a feminine word, the pronoun MUST end in 'i'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Aap gaadi kahan hai?' instead of 'Aapki' (Using subject pronoun instead of possessive).
